brief_justification: This is a theoretical essay on scientific explanation models in economics, discussing methodological issues from J.S. Mill through Friedman to complexity science, using the taxonomy of scientific explanations from modern positivism as its analytical framework. It contains no empirical data analysis, no tables, figures, or equations (all counts are zero), and is purely a philosophy-of-science/methodology discussion. Published in the Brazilian Journal of Political Economy, it falls under methodology and formal theory with an essayistic approach.
